title: Attachment Type Analysis
spl:
index=security tag=email
| rex field=file_name "(?<ext>\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+)$"
| stats count by ext
| sort -count
description: Tracks `file_name` extensions / MIME for macro and archive risk.
value: Tracks `file_name` extensions / MIME for macro and archive risk.
implementation: Alert on `.iso`, `.lnk` spikes from external senders.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email.All_Email by All_Email.action, All_Email.src_user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Mail-volume and content shifts from marketing pushes, O365 throttling, mailbox migrations, and journaling changes; not every spike is a phishing wave.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Email CIM.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `file_name`, `signature`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Alert on `.iso`, `.lnk` spikes from external senders.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=email
| rex field=file_name "(?<ext>\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+)$"
| stats count by ext
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Attachment Type Analysis** — Tracks `file_name` extensions / MIME for macro and archive risk.

Documented **Data sources**: `file_name`, `signature`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Email CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ext** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email.All_Email by All_Email.action, All_Email.src_user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Attachment Type Analysis** — Tracks `file_name` extensions / MIME for macro and archive risk.

Documented **Data sources**: `file_name`, `signature`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Email CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Email.All_Email` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (extensions), Pie chart.
grandmaExplanation: We use the same kind of check described for "Attachment Type Analysis" once our data is shaped in a common, repeatable way across products. We help the team see trouble early, compare different tools fairly, and avoid building a one-off report for every vendor.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Email.All_Email` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
